title: Information Diet

content.question: What's in your information diet that's pure junk, but you consume daily?
sop: Audit every regular input channel, feeds, newsletters, podcasts, people you follow
Rate each source's quality and how much time it consumes (see calculation, page 6)
Unsubscribe or mute the bottom half, the ones that don't inform a decision or change a belief
Build a 'knowledge queue' for the good sources so you consume them intentionally, not reactively
Re-audit quarterly, what was useful six months ago may just be noise now
content.explainer: Insight doesn't come from volume of consumption, it comes from quality multiplied by the time you actually give it, which means a mediocre source you check five times a day can crowd out a genuinely good one you only have time for once a week. Curating inputs isn't about consuming less information, it's about protecting the time budget for the sources that are actually worth it.
questions: What's in your information diet that's pure junk, but you consume daily?
If you tracked your input sources for a week, what percentage would you actually be proud of?
What's one source you keep consuming out of habit, not because it's ever changed a decision you made?
content.equation_example: Rate Quality 1-10. Source A: Quality 8, Time spent 2 hours per week gives an insight contribution of 16. Source B: Quality 2, Time spent 6 hours per week gives an insight contribution of 12, but at 3 times the time cost. Higher insight-per-hour means the source earns its place; a high time cost paired with low quality means it's crowding out better inputs.
content.closing_statement: A low-quality input still costs you real time.

caption:
Principle Nº 226: Information Diet

Curate inputs. Insight doesn't come from volume of consumption, it comes from quality multiplied by the time you actually give it, which means a mediocre source you check five times a day can crowd out a genuinely good one you only have time for once a week.

Ask yourself: What's in your information diet that's pure junk, but you consume daily?
